description: Phase 10 T3 linchpin: C++ BNNSGraphCompileFromFile + BNNSGraphContextExecute on an fp16 fc1 mlmodelc (M=2048,K=1280,N=5120), Apple M3 Pro. Proves the fp16 AMX win is reachable from the engine in pure C++ (no Python/coremltools at runtime).

result.correctness: output bit-matches the CoreML-predict reference (max rel err 0.0000)

foundation: tools/spike_fp16_mlmodelc_gen.py (coremltools 3.12 artifact gen) + tools/spike_bnns_graph_execute.cpp (C++ BNNSGraph runtime). Reference IO via the Python predict.
remaining_integration_scope: Offline per-Linear fp16 mlmodelc generation at convert time (coremltools is 3.12-only; the 3.14 runtime needs NO coremltools — C++ BNNSGraph loads the compiled artifact directly); ship/cache ~132 artifacts (~1.3GB) for 650M; C++ BNNSGraph contexts cached per-Linear at Model::load and executed in the forward via the LinearInt8/Linear dispatch under ESM_APPLE_AMX; dynamic-M for varlen via BNNSGraphContextSetDynamicShapes (spike used fixed M); fp16 PPPL/ProteinGym validation at 150M+650M before ship. De-risked: compile+execute+speed+correctness all proven.
note: T8 (SDOT branch-hoist, ~20% faster SDOT) shrank the fp16 advantage from ~2.3x to ~1.97x over the NEW SDOT, but it remains a real ~2x GEMM lever -> ~1.4x e2e on top of the certain wins.
